import pythonbible as pb
refs = pb.get_references\('1 Chronicles 17'\)
print\('refs:', refs\)
book = refs[0].book
print\('book:', book\)
print\('book.name:', book.name\)
print\('book.name.replace\(\\"_\\",\\" \\"\).title\(\):', book.name.replace\('_', ' '\).title\(\)\)
reconstructed = book.name.replace\('_', ' '\).title\(\) + ' 17:1'
print\('reconstructed full_ref:', reconstructed\)
parsed = pb.get_references\(reconstructed\)
print\('parsed result:', parsed\)
")

Bash(cd "/Users/nickbui/Documents/code/Non-School-CS-Related/Spring 2026/horeb" && uv run python -c "
import pythonbible as pb
broken = []
for book in pb.Book:
    name = book.name.replace\('_', ' '\).title\(\)
    test_ref = name + ' 1:1'
    parsed = pb.get_references\(test_ref\)
    if not parsed:
        broken.append\(\(book.name, name\)\)
print\('Books with broken name reconstruction:'\)
for enum_name, reconstructed in broken:
    print\(f'  {enum_name!r} -> {reconstructed!r}'\)
")
